text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise elderly man who lived alone in a cozy cabin. He had a pet raccoon named Rusty. One autumn day, a storm arrived suddenly. The wind howled, and rain fell heavily. The old man sat by the window watching the swirling grey clouds.

Just then, a bright little pumpkin dropped from nowhere. It landed squarely on the windowsill just outside his reach. The old man reached out a shaky hand and grabbed it. Inside was a note. It read: "Come visit soon!"

The old man smiled. He knew exactly where he'd go when he felt lonely. He explored local farms. He dug up potatoes and listened to the birds. Every visit made him feel less alone.

Years passed, and friends began to die. But memories lingered. Finally, he found himself feeling blue. He missed his adventure. Then he remembered the little pumpkin.

Maybe I should go hunting. No one else I know ever finds such a thing.
